message:
refactor(doctor): expand root .gitignore offending-pattern detection and unify repair messaging

The previous doctor check only flagged two gitignore patterns (.beads/.gitignore
and .beads/*), missing common real-world patterns like .beads/, /.beads/, and
various wildcard forms that hide the inner .beads/.gitignore from git. This caused
bd-to-br migrations to leave broken gitignore state that `br doctor --repair`
couldn't detect or fix.

Changes:
- Add ROOT_GITIGNORE_OFFENDING_PATTERNS constant covering 10 pattern variants
  (directory forms, wildcard forms, bare .beads, explicit .beads/.gitignore paths)
- New is_offending_root_gitignore_pattern() helper centralizes detection, properly
  skipping comments, blanks, and negation (!) lines
- fix_root_gitignore_if_warned() now returns bool so callers know if repair occurred
- After successful gitignore repair, doctor report is re-collected so post-repair
  state is accurate instead of stale
- New repair_outcome_message() composes final message from three independent parts:
  gitignore repair, local index repair, and incomplete REINDEX — replacing scattered
  string literals with named constants
- JSON output "repaired" field now accounts for gitignore-only fixes
  (gitignore_repaired || repair.applied())
- Three new tests: directory pattern warning detection, end-to-end offending pattern
  removal, and combined gitignore + incomplete REINDEX message composition

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
